title: DeepSeek V4 Launches with Huawei Ascend Integration and 73% Cost Reduction — China's AI Independence Stack Matures
detail: DeepSeek released a preview of its V4 model series on April 24, 2026 — one year after R1 disrupted global AI markets in January 2025. The flagship V4-Pro features 1.6 trillion parameters and a 1 million token context window (up from 128K in V3), with validated compatibility on both Nvidia GPU and Huawei Ascend NPU platforms. Huawei issued a separate statement confirming its Ascend 950 chips fully support V4, with its 'Supernode' technology combining large Ascend 950 clusters underpinning inference infrastructure. DeepSeek published API pricing showing a ~73% inference cost reduction vs V3: V4-Pro costs 1 yuan/M tokens (cached input), 12 yuan/M uncached, and 24 yuan/M output; the lighter V4-Flash costs 0.2/1/2 yuan per million tokens. The launch triggered a 9% surge in SMIC shares and a 15% jump in Hua Hong Semiconductor in Hong Kong trading, reflecting investor confidence in accelerating domestic chip demand. DeepSeek remains fully open-source under a permissive license, continuing its strategy of winning global AI distribution. The launch arrived one day after Commerce Secretary Lutnick's Senate confirmation of zero Nvidia H200 chip purchases by China — together, these events validate China's deliberate strategy of building competitive AI capability entirely within the domestic hardware ecosystem. DeepSeek's V4 Ascend validation closes the loop: China is not merely blocked from US chips, but has engineered a complete domestic AI stack where the model, hardware, and inference infrastructure are co-designed outside US supply chains.

media.caption: DeepSeek V4 launches one year after R1 disrupted global AI markets — now running natively on Huawei Ascend and priced 73% below V3

title: Auto China 2026 Opens in Beijing — Chinese EV Makers Showcase In-House AI Chips; Xpeng Unveils Flying Car, Humanoid Robot, and Robotaxi
detail: The 19th Beijing International Automotive Exhibition (Auto China 2026) opened April 24 under the theme 'Future of Intelligence', drawing a record 380,000 sq meter exhibition area — the largest edition in the show's history, running through May 3. Chinese EV makers including BYD, Xpeng, Li Auto, Geely, and Leapmotor showcased in-house AI chips designed to reduce Nvidia dependence in automotive applications. Xpeng's press showcase was the most dramatic: the company unveiled its 'Land Aircraft Carrier' flying car (mass production targeted 2027), the next-gen IRON humanoid robot (production targeted Q4 2026), and the GX robotaxi prototype — China's first factory-integrated Level 4 autonomous vehicle designed for mass production. The GX uses four in-house Turing chips delivering 3,000 TOPS and runs Xpeng's VLA 2.0 physical AI system; 98.52% of new Xpeng owners activated intelligent driving features in their first week. Horizon Robotics launched its Starry 6 processor, integrating cockpit and driving functions into a single SoC. Pony.ai outlined plans for its lower-cost Gen-7 robotaxi vehicles. The show crystallizes China's transition from EV manufacturer to integrated AI-hardware-mobility competitor with companies vertically integrating chip design, AI models, robotics, and vehicle platforms — challenging both Tesla's FSD ambitions and Nvidia's automotive AI chip dominance simultaneously.

media.caption: Auto China 2026 opens in Beijing — Chinese automakers showcase in-house AI chips as global EV competition intensifies
